title: Dai Dai
summary: The headline 2026 World Cup song entry from Shakira and Burna Boy, useful for users looking for the main tournament song rather than the broader album.

timelineNote: The 2026 cycle starts with a headline global-pop entry built for the expanded North American tournament.

title: DNA
artist: Andrea Bocelli, David Guetta, EJAE and Megan Thee Stallion
summary: A focused anthem page for users who want to know whether DNA is the official anthem, who performs it, and how it fits into the broader 2026 soundtrack.
whyItMatters: Users often confuse official song, official anthem, campaign song, and fan chant. This page can answer the distinction quickly, then point to official listening and tournament sources.
timelineNote: A clean example of why the site separates anthem, album track, official song, and fan chant labels.

title: Game Time

title: Por Ella

facts: Useful for users browsing the 2026 album by host country, language, or artist.

title: Muchachos
summary: Argentina's supporter anthem from the Qatar cycle became closely associated with Lionel Messi, Diego Maradona, the Copa America win, and the country's 2022 title run.
whyItMatters: This is the model fan-anthem page: it has a clear country connection, a widely reported tournament moment, and strong evergreen search intent around meaning, origin, and why Argentina fans sing it.
searchAngles: what song do Argentina fans sing
story: Muchachos is valuable because it behaves like a living fan-culture page rather than a static song listing: people search for the meaning, the Argentina connection, and why it became attached to the 2022 title run.
facts: This page should explain the chant without reproducing lyrics.
It belongs in fan-anthem browsing, not in a list of FIFA official songs.
timelineNote: A fan anthem became one of the defining sounds of Argentina's 2022 title run.

title: Three Lions
artist: Baddiel, Skinner and The Lightning Seeds
summary: Three Lions is not a FIFA World Cup official song, but its 'coming home' refrain has become one of the most recognizable sounds around England at major tournaments.
whyItMatters: This page is useful because users often ask what England fans mean by 'it's coming home' and whether Three Lions is an official World Cup song. It is also less time-sensitive than a single-match chant.
searchAngles: why do England fans sing Three Lions
what does football's coming home mean
is Three Lions a World Cup song
story: Three Lions is a strong example of tournament music outgrowing its original release window. For World Cup searches, the useful answer is usually the distinction between England fan culture and FIFA official music.
facts: Treat it as an unofficial England anthem rather than a FIFA World Cup official song.
Good for glossary links explaining fan anthem, chant, and official song.
timelineNote: The clearest proof that a tournament song can become bigger as fan culture than as an official release.

title: Take Me Home, Country Roads
summary: During the 2026 tournament, USMNT fans and players were reported celebrating with Country Roads after a win, creating a current-cycle example of how a familiar song can become a matchday soundtrack.
whyItMatters: This is a deliberately small trend page: useful for testing whether current fan-moment explainers can earn search impressions, while keeping the wording sourced and cautious.
searchAngles: why are USMNT fans singing Country Roads
timelineNote: A current-cycle fan moment showing how familiar songs can attach themselves to a specific team run.

title: Waka Waka
summary: One of the most durable World Cup music search anchors, remembered for its 2010 South Africa context and global pop reach.
whyItMatters: Every tournament brings users back to Waka Waka. A detail page should answer year, artist, tournament, official status, language variants, and why it remains culturally sticky.
story: Waka Waka is the benchmark evergreen page for this site: users return to it every tournament because they remember the song before they remember the exact tournament metadata.
facts: A useful detail page should connect the song to South Africa 2010, Shakira, Freshlyground, and official listening links.
Do not reproduce lyrics; the value is context, status, and safe outbound links.
timelineNote: The modern World Cup pop reference point: official, globally remembered, and rediscovered every tournament.

title: Wavin' Flag
summary: A famous World Cup-adjacent song that many users remember from 2010, even though it is often discussed separately from the official FIFA song.
whyItMatters: This is a perfect confusion page: users search whether Wavin' Flag was official, why it was everywhere, and how it relates to Waka Waka.
timelineNote: A perfect confusion node: widely remembered from 2010, but searched alongside the official-song conversation.

title: The Cup of Life

searchAngles: La Copa de la Vida World Cup
story: The Cup of Life is a useful older anchor because many users search by performer first, then discover the 1998 tournament connection and the Spanish title La Copa de la Vida.
facts: Good internal links: 1998, France, Ricky Martin, La Copa de la Vida.
The page should help users distinguish the English title from the Spanish title without copying lyrics.
